top10.introHtml: Ranked for a cheap fun second car — condition over miles. Preference for reliable platforms (Solstice/Sky, Miata, BMW inline-6), low-mileage survivors, working top, and no rust or salvage history. Manual preferred. All picks within 35 miles of Media, PA (ZIP 19063). Refreshed May 14, 2026. Sources: Cars.com (24 listings, clean-title+no-accident filter), CarGurus (112 total), AutoTrader (99 total). TrueCar URL broken on first run — fix slug for next refresh.

picks.whyGood: Only 37,018 miles on a 2008 — this top, headliner, and rear window have barely been stressed. At 9 miles from Media it is practically a local car. CarGurus Great Deal badge confirms it is priced below market. The base Kappa is still genuinely fun to drive and 37k miles means the top rubber and seals are near-new.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top through 3+ full open/close cycles and listen for motor hesitation; check the header latches positively. Inspect the rear plastic window for crazing or seam separation. Check rocker panels and sills for rust (Solstice rockers are notorious). Verify tire age (DOT code) — 30k miles over 17 years may mean extended storage periods. Check brake fluid and coolant condition.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-mileage fun car in the batch — see this one first.

picks.whyGood: GXP means the turbocharged 260hp 2.0L Ecotec — a completely different car from the base Solstice. At $8,195 with 88k miles and a Great Deal badge on CarGurus (same car listed at $8,590), this is the best price-to-fun ratio in the entire batch. No accidents, clean title confirmed via Cars.com filter.
picks.whatToVerify: Top through full cycles plus rocker rust check (same as all Solstice variants). Additionally: verify boost holds under hard acceleration (LNF wastegate can rattle), check for oil consumption between changes, inspect intercooler couplings for cracks. Timing chain is oil-pressure sensitive — confirm regular oil change history.
picks.bottomLine: Best bang-for-buck turbo roadster in the batch — GXP at this price is genuinely a deal.

picks.whyGood: The Saturn Sky is the Solstice's twin on the Kappa platform with arguably sportier styling. At 30,483 miles this is an extraordinary time capsule — top rubber, interior, and seals should all be in near-new condition. Fair Deal at $12,995 is reasonable given the mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: Same top and rocker checks as Solstice. With only 30k miles over 18 years expect possible long-storage issues: verify tire date codes, brake fluid freshness, and coolant condition. Run to full operating temp to confirm thermostat function.
picks.bottomLine: If the Solstice is gone, this is its best twin — 30k miles at this price is exceptional.

picks.whyGood: The NC-generation Miata (2006-2015) is the most reliable, refined, and parts-supported sports convertible under $15k. 82k miles is barely middle age for an NC. The Touring trim adds leather, heated seats, and a Bose stereo. No accidents reported. Dealer listing from a Lexus store suggests a proper trade-in inspection.
picks.whatToVerify: Top through full open/close cycle — NC tops are robust but check the rear window for lower-edge delamination. Look for oil seepage at the valve cover and oil pan. Confirm no timing chain service is overdue. Check headliner and rear carpet corners for water-stain evidence.
picks.bottomLine: The safest, most durable pick in the batch — Miata ownership rarely disappoints.

picks.whyGood: The E88 128i carries BMW's N52 naturally-aspirated 3.0L inline-6 — one of the smoothest, most reliable BMW engines ever, unlike the turbocharged N54/N55 in the 135i. At $9,500 with 69k miles and a Great Deal badge this is significantly underpriced. The 128i convertible is rare and underappreciated.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft-top hydraulic rams are the main E88 weakness — operate through 5+ full cycles and listen for pump hesitation or incomplete travel. Check rear window for delamination. N52 oil leaks: valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket are the usual suspects. Cooling system: confirm water pump and thermostat have been addressed.
picks.bottomLine: A proper BMW drop-top at Chrysler money — the N52 engine makes this an excellent long-term bet.

picks.whyGood: The E46 325Ci is widely regarded as one of the best-handling BMWs ever. 60,200 miles on a 2001 is exceptional — barely driven over 24 years. No accidents, clean title from Cars.com filter. The M54 inline-6 is smooth, tuneful, and well-proven.
picks.whatToVerify: E46 soft-top hydraulic pump leaks are common — check under the left rear trunk corner. Subframe rear mount bushings: E46 subframe cracking is a known issue, look for cracking at the rear subframe mounts. Cooling system: water pump, thermostat, expansion tank. Oil leaks at valve cover and VANOS unit.
picks.bottomLine: For driving character the E46 325Ci is hard to beat — budget for deferred maintenance on a 24-year-old car.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 2.8 with the M52 straight-six is night-and-day better than the four-cylinder Z3. 75k miles, no accidents, very close to Media. The Z3 roadster top is a simple manually-latched fabric unit — nothing electronic to fail. Classic proportions and a great weekend road car.
picks.whatToVerify: Top condition and fold-seam brittleness — Z3 tops age and replacement is affordable but worth budgeting. M52 cooling system: thermostat and water pump are common failures. Inspect rocker panels and floor for rust. Check rear window for delamination.
picks.bottomLine: The most classically pretty roadster in the batch — simple, close, and affordable.

picks.whyGood: Second GXP turbo in the top 10 for sourcing diversity. At 51,730 miles this is the lowest-mileage GXP in the batch — better-preserved than the rank-2 example at a higher but still fair price. 20 miles from Media.
picks.whatToVerify: Same as rank-2 GXP: full top cycle, rocker rust, LNF boost pressure, oil consumption check, timing chain maintenance history.
picks.bottomLine: If you want the lowest-mileage GXP, this is it — premium provenance at a premium price.

picks.whyGood: Base Solstice (naturally aspirated 2.4L Ecotec) at $7,500 with 62k miles is the budget roadster pick. The non-turbo drivetrain is less fussy than the GXP. 62k miles is very reasonable on the Kappa platform. Good sourcing diversity from AutoTrader.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle, rocker rust inspection (critical on all Solstice variants), confirm no unreported accident damage, verify 5-speed shifts cleanly when cold.
picks.bottomLine: Entry-level Kappa fun at a fair price — the non-turbo means fewer things to worry about.

picks.whyGood: Under 30,000 miles on a 2010 MINI is remarkable — essentially a lightly used car. Cooper S adds the turbocharged 1.6T engine for real performance over the base. Clean title, no accidents. Genuinely fun and distinctive as a second car.
picks.whatToVerify: MINI 1.6T Prince engine timing chain tensioner is a known weak point — listen for cold-start chain rattle and ask for service records. Valve cover oil leaks. Electric water pump can fail without warning — check coolant level and condition. Full top cycle including header seal compression. Check footwells for water after rain.
picks.bottomLine: The wildcard pick — almost no miles and quirky fun, but do the timing chain homework before buying.
